keyConcepts.title: Algorithm Efficiency
keyConcepts.description: Understand that multiple algorithms can solve the same problem but require different amounts of time or memory.

keyConcepts.title: Input Size
keyConcepts.description: Recognize n as a representation of how much data an algorithm needs to process.

keyConcepts.title: Growth Rates
keyConcepts.description: Compare algorithms based on how their resource needs increase as the input becomes larger.

keyConcepts.title: Constant Time
keyConcepts.description: Recognize O(1) operations whose cost does not grow with the size of the input.

keyConcepts.title: Linear Time
keyConcepts.description: Recognize O(n) algorithms that may need to process each item once.

keyConcepts.title: Logarithmic Time
keyConcepts.description: Understand O(log n) algorithms that repeatedly reduce the remaining problem size, such as binary search.

keyConcepts.title: Quadratic Time
keyConcepts.description: Recognize O(n²) algorithms that often involve nested loops over the same collection.

keyConcepts.title: Common Complexities
keyConcepts.description: Become familiar with common complexity categories such as O(1), O(log n), O(n), O(n log n), and O(n²).

keyConcepts.title: Dominant Terms
keyConcepts.description: Understand why Big O notation focuses on the fastest-growing part of an expression and ignores constant factors.

keyConcepts.title: Time and Space
keyConcepts.description: Distinguish between time complexity, which estimates work performed, and space complexity, which estimates additional memory usage.

learningResources.title: OpenDSA: Asymptotic Analysis and Upper Bounds
learningResources.description: An educational module explaining growth rates, upper bounds, and Big O notation.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It introduces algorithm analysis carefully and includes examples that connect notation to practical performance.

learningResources.title: CS50: Week 3 Algorithms
learningResources.description: Harvard's free CS50 lesson introduces searching, sorting, recursion, and asymptotic notation.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It explains Big O notation in the context of algorithms that beginners can visualize and compare.

learningResources.title: Khan Academy: Functions in Asymptotic Notation
learningResources.description: A free lesson comparing common mathematical growth rates used during algorithm analysis.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It helps learners recognize how common complexities relate to one another before analyzing code.

learningResources.title: freeCodeCamp: Time Complexity - Big O Notation Course
learningResources.description: A free course covering common time complexities, space complexity, binary search, and merge sort.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It uses diagrams and coding examples to make an abstract topic more approachable.

keyConcepts.title: Sequential Storage
keyConcepts.description: Understand that an array stores a sequence of elements in a defined order.

keyConcepts.title: Indexes
keyConcepts.description: Access an array element using its numerical position, typically starting at index zero.

keyConcepts.title: Array Length
keyConcepts.description: Determine how many elements are stored in an array.

keyConcepts.title: Reading Elements
keyConcepts.description: Retrieve values efficiently when the index of an element is known.

keyConcepts.title: Updating Elements
keyConcepts.description: Replace the value stored at a specific index.

keyConcepts.title: Traversing Arrays
keyConcepts.description: Use a loop to process each element in order.

keyConcepts.title: Insertion and Removal
keyConcepts.description: Understand why inserting or removing an element can require shifting other elements.

keyConcepts.title: Fixed and Dynamic Arrays
keyConcepts.description: Recognize the difference between fixed-size arrays and resizable array structures such as dynamic arrays.

keyConcepts.title: Operation Complexity
keyConcepts.description: Compare the typical costs of indexed access, searching, insertion, and deletion.

keyConcepts.title: Array Use Cases
keyConcepts.description: Identify situations where ordered data and fast access by index make arrays a useful choice.

learningResources.title: VisuAlgo: Array
learningResources.description: An interactive visualization of array operations and the relationship between arrays and sorted arrays.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It allows learners to see how values are accessed, updated, inserted, and removed.

learningResources.title: OpenDSA: Chapter Introduction to Lists
learningResources.description: An educational introduction to list structures and the differences between logical representations and physical implementations.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It prepares learners to compare array-based lists with linked lists and understand their tradeoffs.

learningResources.title: CS50: Week 5 Data Structures
learningResources.description: Harvard's free CS50 lesson explains foundational data structures and how their memory layouts affect performance.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It helps learners connect array concepts to later topics such as linked lists and hash tables.

learningResources.title: freeCodeCamp: Data Structures Explained
learningResources.description: A free language-agnostic course introducing arrays and other important data structures.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It provides diagrams and examples without requiring expertise in a specific programming language.

keyConcepts.title: Nodes
keyConcepts.description: Understand that a linked list is built from individual nodes that store data.

keyConcepts.title: Next References
keyConcepts.description: Recognize that each node connects to the next node through a reference or pointer.

keyConcepts.title: Head Node
keyConcepts.description: Understand that the head reference identifies the first node in the list.

keyConcepts.title: List Traversal
keyConcepts.description: Move from one node to the next to access or search the values stored in the list.

keyConcepts.title: Node Insertion
keyConcepts.description: Insert a new node by updating the references between existing nodes.

keyConcepts.title: Node Removal
keyConcepts.description: Remove a node by reconnecting the surrounding references.

keyConcepts.title: Singly Linked Lists
keyConcepts.description: Understand a list structure where each node stores a reference to only the next node.

keyConcepts.title: Doubly Linked Lists
keyConcepts.description: Recognize that a doubly linked list stores references to both the next and previous nodes.

keyConcepts.title: Boundary Cases
keyConcepts.description: Handle operations involving an empty list or changes to the first and last nodes.

keyConcepts.title: Array Tradeoffs
keyConcepts.description: Compare linked lists with arrays in terms of traversal, indexed access, memory usage, insertion, and deletion.

learningResources.title: VisuAlgo: Linked List, Stack, Queue, and Deque
learningResources.description: An interactive visualization showing linked-list nodes, references, insertion, removal, traversal, stacks, queues, and deques.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It makes pointer updates visible, which helps beginners understand operations that can be difficult to picture from code alone.

learningResources.title: OpenDSA: Linked Lists
learningResources.description: An educational module explaining nodes, next references, dynamic allocation, and linked-list implementation.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It provides diagrams and implementation details while keeping the focus on core linked-list ideas.

learningResources.title: CS50: Week 5 Data Structures
learningResources.description: Harvard's CS50 lesson includes short videos and lecture materials on singly linked lists and doubly linked lists.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It explains why linked lists exist and demonstrates how they differ from arrays.

learningResources.title: freeCodeCamp: Data Structures Handbook
learningResources.description: A beginner-friendly handbook introducing arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, and graphs.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It reinforces the purpose and real-world relevance of linked lists through approachable explanations.

keyConcepts.title: Abstract Data Types
keyConcepts.description: Understand that stacks and queues describe rules for adding and removing values rather than one specific implementation.

keyConcepts.title: Stack LIFO Order
keyConcepts.description: Recognize that a stack follows last-in, first-out behavior.

keyConcepts.title: Push and Pop
keyConcepts.description: Add an item to the top of a stack and remove the most recently added item.

keyConcepts.title: Stack Peek
keyConcepts.description: Inspect the item at the top of a stack without removing it.

keyConcepts.title: Queue FIFO Order
keyConcepts.description: Recognize that a queue follows first-in, first-out behavior.

keyConcepts.title: Enqueue and Dequeue
keyConcepts.description: Add an item to the back of a queue and remove the item at the front.

keyConcepts.title: Queue Front
keyConcepts.description: Inspect the next queue item that would be removed.

keyConcepts.title: Array Implementations
keyConcepts.description: Understand how arrays can store stack and queue values while tracking relevant positions.

keyConcepts.title: Linked Implementations
keyConcepts.description: Understand how linked nodes can support efficient stack and queue operations.

keyConcepts.title: Common Use Cases
keyConcepts.description: Recognize stacks in undo features and function calls, and queues in scheduling and task processing.

learningResources.title: VisuAlgo: Linked List, Stack, Queue, and Deque
learningResources.description: An interactive visualization for exploring linked lists, stacks, queues, and deques.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It visually demonstrates LIFO and FIFO behavior as learners add and remove values.

learningResources.title: OpenDSA: Stacks
learningResources.description: An educational module explaining stack terminology and array-based stack implementation.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It clearly connects stack operations such as push and pop to an underlying data structure.

learningResources.title: CS50: Week 5 Data Structures
learningResources.description: Harvard's CS50 lesson includes videos and lecture materials on stacks, queues, and other foundational structures.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It explains the concepts with concrete examples before learners attempt implementations.

learningResources.title: freeCodeCamp: Learn Algorithms and Data Structures in Python
learningResources.description: A free beginner course covering linked lists, stacks, queues, recursion, searching, and sorting.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It gives learners a practical way to see stacks and queues implemented in a familiar programming language.

keyConcepts.title: Key-Value Pairs
keyConcepts.description: Understand that a hash map stores values that can be retrieved using associated keys.

keyConcepts.title: Hash Functions
keyConcepts.description: Understand that a hash function transforms a key into an index used to locate stored data.

keyConcepts.title: Buckets
keyConcepts.description: Recognize that a hash table uses storage locations, often called buckets or slots.

keyConcepts.title: Insert Operations
keyConcepts.description: Store a key-value pair in the location determined by a hash function.

keyConcepts.title: Lookup Operations
keyConcepts.description: Retrieve a stored value by hashing its key and checking the corresponding location.

keyConcepts.title: Delete Operations
keyConcepts.description: Remove a key-value pair while preserving the behavior of later lookups.

keyConcepts.title: Hash Collisions
keyConcepts.description: Understand that different keys can produce the same storage index.

keyConcepts.title: Collision Resolution
keyConcepts.description: Recognize common strategies such as separate chaining and open addressing for handling collisions.

keyConcepts.title: Average Complexity
keyConcepts.description: Understand why insertions, lookups, and deletions are commonly expected to take O(1) time on average.

keyConcepts.title: Load Factor
keyConcepts.description: Recognize that hash-table performance can decline when too many values are stored relative to the number of available buckets.

learningResources.title: VisuAlgo: Hash Table
learningResources.description: An interactive visualization of hashing, insertion, removal, searching, collisions, chaining, and open addressing.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It allows learners to see exactly what happens when multiple keys map to the same location.

learningResources.title: OpenDSA: Introduction to Hashing
learningResources.description: An educational introduction to hash tables, search keys, storage locations, and hash functions.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It explains why hash maps are useful before introducing implementation details.

learningResources.title: CS50: Week 5 Data Structures
learningResources.description: Harvard's CS50 lesson includes lecture materials and short videos explaining hash tables.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It connects hash tables to arrays and linked lists, helping learners build on topics they already understand.

learningResources.title: freeCodeCamp: What Is a Hash Map?
learningResources.description: A practical guide explaining hash maps, common operations, time complexity, and a coding-problem example.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It reinforces the concept with approachable examples and explains both average-case and worst-case performance.

keyConcepts.title: Self-Calling Functions
keyConcepts.description: Understand that a recursive function solves part of a problem by calling itself.

keyConcepts.title: Smaller Subproblems
keyConcepts.description: Break a larger problem into one or more smaller versions of the same problem.

keyConcepts.title: Base Cases
keyConcepts.description: Define a stopping condition that returns a result without making another recursive call.

keyConcepts.title: Recursive Cases
keyConcepts.description: Define how the function reduces the problem and continues toward a base case.

keyConcepts.title: Progress Toward Termination
keyConcepts.description: Ensure that every recursive step moves closer to the stopping condition.

keyConcepts.title: Call Stack
keyConcepts.description: Understand that each recursive call creates a new stack frame that waits for its result.

keyConcepts.title: Tracing Calls
keyConcepts.description: Follow recursive calls forward and return values backward to understand execution order.

keyConcepts.title: Infinite Recursion
keyConcepts.description: Recognize that missing or unreachable base cases can cause a program to exceed its call-stack limit.

keyConcepts.title: Recursive Patterns
keyConcepts.description: Recognize recursion in tasks such as factorial calculations, tree traversal, divide-and-conquer algorithms, and nested structures.

keyConcepts.title: Iteration Tradeoffs
keyConcepts.description: Compare recursive and iterative solutions based on readability, memory usage, and problem structure.

learningResources.title: OpenDSA: Introduction to Recursion
learningResources.description: An educational lesson introducing recursive functions as solutions that reduce problems into smaller subproblems.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It establishes the core mental model learners need before writing recursive code.

learningResources.title: OpenDSA: Writing a Recursive Function
learningResources.description: A guided lesson explaining how to design recursive solutions step by step.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It helps learners move from understanding the idea of recursion to writing their own functions.

learningResources.title: OpenDSA: Recursion Practice Exercises
learningResources.description: A collection of exercises for practicing recursive thinking and code completion.
learningResources.beginnerValue: Recursion becomes easier through repeated tracing and practice, and this resource provides a structured sequence of exercises.

learningResources.title: CS50: Week 3 Algorithms
learningResources.description: Harvard's free CS50 lesson introduces recursion alongside searching and sorting algorithms.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It demonstrates recursion in an algorithmic context rather than presenting it as an isolated syntax feature.

keyConcepts.title: Sorting Purpose
keyConcepts.description: Understand that sorting rearranges values or records into a defined order.

keyConcepts.title: Comparison Operations
keyConcepts.description: Recognize that many sorting algorithms determine order by comparing pairs of values.

keyConcepts.title: Swap Operations
keyConcepts.description: Understand how sorting algorithms move values into different positions.

keyConcepts.title: Bubble Sort
keyConcepts.description: Understand a basic sorting algorithm that repeatedly swaps neighboring values that are out of order.

keyConcepts.title: Selection Sort
keyConcepts.description: Understand a basic sorting algorithm that repeatedly selects the next smallest or largest value.

keyConcepts.title: Insertion Sort
keyConcepts.description: Understand a sorting algorithm that places each new value into the correct position within an already sorted portion.

keyConcepts.title: Merge Sort
keyConcepts.description: Understand a divide-and-conquer algorithm that splits data, sorts smaller sections, and merges the results.

keyConcepts.title: Sorting Complexity
keyConcepts.description: Compare O(n²) beginner sorting algorithms with more efficient O(n log n) approaches such as merge sort.

keyConcepts.title: Stable Sorting
keyConcepts.description: Recognize that a stable sorting algorithm preserves the original order of items with equal keys.

keyConcepts.title: Algorithm Tradeoffs
keyConcepts.description: Understand that the best sorting choice can depend on the size, existing order, memory limits, and requirements of the data.

learningResources.title: VisuAlgo: Sorting
learningResources.description: An interactive visualization comparing bubble sort, selection sort, insertion sort, merge sort, quicksort, and other sorting algorithms.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It makes comparisons, swaps, and performance differences visible as each algorithm runs.

learningResources.title: CS50: Week 3 Algorithms
learningResources.description: Harvard's free CS50 lesson covers bubble sort, selection sort, merge sort, asymptotic notation, and recursion.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It introduces sorting algorithms in a clear progression from simpler approaches to more efficient ones.

learningResources.title: OpenDSA: Introduction to Sorting
learningResources.description: An educational introduction explaining the sorting problem and why different sorting algorithms exist.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It provides context before learners study individual algorithms and their performance.

learningResources.title: Khan Academy: Computer Science Algorithms
learningResources.description: A free algorithms unit covering selection sort, insertion sort, merge sort, recursion, binary search, and asymptotic notation.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It offers a structured sequence of lessons and exercises that connect sorting algorithms to broader CS concepts.

keyConcepts.title: Search Problems
keyConcepts.description: Understand that a search algorithm attempts to find whether a target value exists and often where it is located.

keyConcepts.title: Linear Search
keyConcepts.description: Check elements one at a time until the target is found or the collection ends.

keyConcepts.title: Linear Complexity
keyConcepts.description: Understand why searching an unsorted array with linear search can require O(n) comparisons.

keyConcepts.title: Sorted Data
keyConcepts.description: Recognize that some faster searching techniques require data to already be ordered.

keyConcepts.title: Binary Search
keyConcepts.description: Find a target in sorted data by checking the middle element and repeatedly discarding half of the remaining search area.

keyConcepts.title: Search Boundaries
keyConcepts.description: Track the low, high, and middle positions while narrowing a binary-search interval.

keyConcepts.title: Logarithmic Complexity
keyConcepts.description: Understand why repeatedly halving a search space gives binary search an O(log n) time complexity.

keyConcepts.title: Missing Values
keyConcepts.description: Return a clear result when the target does not exist in the collection.

keyConcepts.title: Duplicate Values
keyConcepts.description: Recognize that additional logic may be needed when a search should find the first or last occurrence of a repeated value.

keyConcepts.title: Search Tradeoffs
keyConcepts.description: Compare the simplicity of linear search with the speed and sorted-data requirement of binary search.

learningResources.title: OpenDSA: Searching in an Array
learningResources.description: An educational module comparing sequential search with binary search on sorted arrays.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It clearly explains why sorting data enables a faster search strategy.

learningResources.title: CS50: Week 3 Algorithms
learningResources.description: Harvard's free CS50 lesson covers linear search, binary search, sorting, recursion, and asymptotic notation.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It introduces searching with visual examples and directly compares the efficiency of different approaches.

learningResources.title: freeCodeCamp: Binary Search Algorithm and Time Complexity Explained
learningResources.description: A beginner-friendly guide comparing linear search with binary search and explaining their time complexities.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It provides step-by-step code examples and reinforces the requirement that binary-search input must be sorted.

learningResources.title: Khan Academy: Computer Science Algorithms
learningResources.description: A free algorithms unit introducing binary search, sorting, recursion, and asymptotic notation.
learningResources.beginnerValue: It helps learners understand searching as part of a connected progression of foundational algorithm topics.
